entity_boundary_classes.description: Personal sovereign data and operator-system truth with no external tenant boundary.
Creative sovereign work that stays local-first and private.
Case-bound or regulated material with narrow handling rules.
Tax and financial casework with long retention and strict export rules.
Business or client-facing work with explicit counterparty boundaries.
Private product work that may use cloud-safe implementation lanes when allowed.

account_surface_classes.description: Only local/operator-owned accounts and surfaces are allowed.
Operator plus governed runtime services may act inside the same boundary.
Preview, staging, or product accounts may exist but remain governed and scoped.
Client or counterparty workflow accounts are in play and require explicit boundaries.

counterparty_sensitivity_classes.description: No external counterparty sensitivity is expected.
Private creative material that must not drift into shared external surfaces.
Private end-user or tenant data requiring explicit boundaries.
Business or client data requiring confidentiality and review.
Regulated or legally sensitive counterparty data.
Tax or financial counterparty material with long retention and redaction constraints.

notes: Party registry entries may live in higher-level domain or tenant registries; this file defines the required shape.
